title: Meridian UK AIM Growth Portfolio
content:
The Meridian UK AIM Growth Portfolio is a high-conviction equity strategy designed to capture the exceptional growth potential of companies listed on the Alternative Investment Market. This concentrated portfolio targets emerging and mid-tier businesses with market capitalisations typically ranging from £10 million to £500 million, focusing on companies demonstrating sustainable competitive advantages and scalable business models.

PORTFOLIO CONSTRUCTION & RISK PROFILE
The portfolio maintains 25-35 holdings across diversified sectors including technology, healthcare, consumer discretionary, and industrial services. Our investment committee applies rigorous fundamental analysis to identify companies with revenue growth potential exceeding 15% annually over a three-year investment horizon. The strategy targets gross returns of 12-18% per annum, acknowledging that AIM securities carry heightened volatility and liquidity constraints compared to main market equities.

Given the aggressive risk classification, investors should anticipate significant capital fluctuations. Historical analysis indicates potential drawdowns of 25-40% during adverse market conditions. The portfolio's beta coefficient typically ranges between 1.3-1.6 relative to the FTSE AIM All-Share Index, reflecting amplified sensitivity to broader market movements.

FEE STRUCTURE & MINIMUM INVESTMENT
The annual management charge is 1.85% plus VAT, calculated quarterly on the portfolio's average daily value. Performance fees of 15% apply to returns exceeding the FTSE AIM All-Share Index plus 200 basis points, subject to high-water mark provisions. Transaction costs average 0.45% annually, reflecting the higher dealing spreads inherent in AIM securities. Minimum investment threshold is £100,000, with subsequent additions accepted in £10,000 increments.

REGULATORY CONSIDERATIONS
In accordance with FCA COBS 9.2 suitability requirements, this product is exclusively available to advised clients following comprehensive risk assessment. The strategy is unsuitable for investors requiring capital preservation or regular income generation. Advisors must ensure clients understand AIM-specific risks including reduced regulatory oversight, potential illiquidity during market stress, and elevated business failure rates compared to main market companies.

Clients must demonstrate sufficient financial resources to withstand potential total capital loss. The portfolio requires minimum liquid assets of £300,000 beyond the AIM allocation, ensuring adequate diversification within overall investment holdings. Tax considerations include potential Business Property Relief eligibility for AIM securities held over two years, subject to individual circumstances and current legislation.

PERFORMANCE ATTRIBUTION & OUTLOOK
Since inception in March 2019, the portfolio has delivered gross annualised returns of 14.7%, outperforming the FTSE AIM All-Share by 380 basis points annually. Technology holdings contributed 42% of outperformance, particularly software-as-a-service businesses demonstrating recurring revenue models. Healthcare investments, representing 28% of current allocation, have benefited from increased digitalisation trends and demographic tailwinds.

Current market positioning reflects cautious optimism regarding UK smaller company valuations following recent currency stabilisation and reduced political uncertainty. The investment committee anticipates continued outperformance from companies with strong export capabilities, particularly those serving North American and European markets. However, investors should remain cognisant of macroeconomic headwinds including elevated interest rates and potential recessionary pressures affecting growth company valuations.

This factsheet summarises key investment characteristics for professional advisory use only. Detailed risk disclosures and current portfolio holdings are available through the client portal. All performance data assumes reinvestment of dividends and excludes advisor fees.
